items.tldr: Overnight flight to Seoul — follow the jet-lag protocol on board.
items.title: Flight — depart Newark (EWR) at ≈01:00 EDT
items.pace: Travel day · depart EWR ≈01:00 EDT · ~15 hrs in the air · land ICN Terminal 1 ≈04:55 KST Jul 9

waypoints.name: Depart EWR Terminal B (Air Premia YP132)
waypoints.note: ⚠ Confirm time against your booking

waypoints.name: Land ICN Terminal 1
waypoints.note: e-Arrival QR → T-money at arrivals

items.note: The jet lag protocol in the Plan section covers exactly what to do on this flight. Follow it.
items.body:
A night flight across the Pacific — the trip starts in the dark and ends with a Korean sunrise.

  • Depart EWR ≈01:00 EDT (Terminal B). Air Premia YP132 — ≈14–15 hrs, land ICN Terminal 1 ≈04:55 KST Thu Jul 9 (⚠ confirm times against your booking).
  • On the plane: Eye mask + earplugs immediately. No alcohol. Water every hour (~250 ml). Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at ~02:00 EDT. Window shade closed — polar route has near-continuous daylight. Eat the breakfast service near landing.
  • On landing: e-Arrival Card QR at immigration → T-money card at arrivals → AREX All-Stop to Hongik University Station (≈49 min, ≈₩4,050 — no transfer). See 'When you land' checklist for full steps.
items.fit: Compression socks; something easy to sleep in on the plane.
checklist: e-Arrival Card QR downloaded and saved offline
Melatonin packed in carry-on (0.5–1 mg for ~02:00 EDT)
Battery pack and all devices fully charged
Eye mask and earplugs in carry-on
Depart EWR — not JFK

items.tldr: Shower at ICN, bus to Jongno, drop bags, stay awake in daylight, jimjilbang, bed by 22:30.
items.title: Arrive Seoul — light, bathhouse, early night
items.pace: Arrival day · shower at ICN · 6002 to Jongno ~75 min · outdoor light by 09:00 · jimjilbang 17:00–21:00 · bed by 22:30

waypoints.name: ICN T1 — shower, then bus 6002
waypoints.note: Spa on Air B1 · then 6002 counter, ₩17,000 pp

waypoints.name: Jongno base — drop bags (19 Jong-ro)
waypoints.note: Across from Olive Young Jonggak · do not sleep

waypoints.name: Cheonggyecheon / Jongno light walk
waypoints.note: 20 min outdoor light — strongest clock anchor

waypoints.name: SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang
waypoints.note: Jongno 3-ga → Line 5 to DDP H&C Park, Exit 14, B3F

waypoints.name: Dinner near the base — Jongno BBQ
waypoints.note: Walk-in samgyeopsal on Jongno/Insadong · bed by 22:30

items.note: The most important day of the trip for jet lag. Follow the protocol exactly — especially the bedtime.
items.body:
After 15 hours in the air, Seoul delivers exactly what exhausted travellers need: a city that works perfectly at 6 a.m.

  • ~05:30 — shower at ICN. Clear immigration, grab a T-money card, then reset at Spa on Air (B1 by arrivals Door 3, ≈₩10,000/2 hr, towel + toiletries; ⚠ confirm name/price). A hot shower after the redeye is the difference between a usable day and a write-off.
  • ~06:30 — Airport Limousine Bus 6002 → Jongno. Buy at the T1 arrivals bus counter (card, ₩17,000 pp) and board 6002; get off at Gwanghwamun or Jongno 3-ga (~75 min at dawn, no traffic; first bus 05:35). Luggage rides under the bus. From the stop, a ~7-min walk or short Kakao T to the base — 19 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu, across from Olive Young Jonggak. Drop bags — do not sleep.
  • ~09:00 — outdoor light. Step out along Cheonggyecheon for 20 min of morning light — the single strongest clock anchor. Spin the dense Jongno PokéStops if you want (Road of Legends live — skip raiding today, save passes for tomorrow).
  • 10:00–17:00 — stay awake. You're steps from Insadong, Cheonggyecheon, and Gwangjang Market — an easy flat loop with an AC café break every 90 min. Goal: awake, upright, in daylight. Don't commit to real sightseeing.
  • 17:00–21:00 — SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang. Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga, then Line 5 two stops to Dongdaemun H&C Park (~12 min) → Exit 14, B3F Goodmorning City (247 Jangchungdan-ro, Jung-gu). Entry ₩12,000 if you enter by 20:00 (≈₩7,000 late night after 20:00 — ⚠ verify on Klook), pay on-site; open 24 h. Heated rooms + cooling breaks. ⚠ Verify tattoo policy on the Klook listing if relevant.
  • ~21:00 — dinner + bed. Walk-in Korean BBQ near the base — samgyeopsal joints line Jongno and Insadong 5 min away (see Where to eat). Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00. In bed by 22:30 — the most important sleep of the trip.
items.fit: Light, breathable clothes. Flip-flops in your bag for the jimjilbang.
checklist: T-money card bought + loaded at ICN arrivals
TravelPass switched on (text TRAVEL to 4004)
Do NOT nap over 30 min — jet lag protocol
Outdoor light before 10:00 KST
SPAREX Dongdaemun: arrive ≈17:00, exit by 21:00 (Dongdaemun H&C Park Station, Exit 14)
Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00 KST
In bed by 22:30 KST

items.tldr: Palaces and old Seoul by day; Road of Legends raid night in Hongdae.
items.title: Palaces & old Seoul
items.pace: Full day · Gyeongbokgung ~2 hr · Bukchon ~1.5 hr · Insadong ~1.5 hr · lots of walking

waypoints.name: Gyeongbokgung
waypoints.note: Guard ceremony ≈10:00 (⚠ verify) · closed Tue

waypoints.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
waypoints.note: Residential — keep voices low

waypoints.name: Insadong
waypoints.note: Tea houses + iced-coffee stop

waypoints.name: LoL Park (Gran Seoul, 33 Jong-ro)
waypoints.note: Jonggak Stn Exit 1 · exhibits free, 45–60 min

waypoints.name: Hongdae raid hours (Dialga/Palkia → Primals)
waypoints.note: Road of Legends finale — 5+ trainers for Primals

waypoints.name: T1 Basecamp (147 Yanghwa-ro, B1)
waypoints.note: Merch shop open until 22:00

items.note: Heat and humidity are the real workload — water, shade breaks, an iced-coffee stop. Your body clock is 1–2 days adjusted at this point.
items.body:
Grand palaces and stone lanes a few minutes from your door, the home of the LCK one stop away, and a Hongdae raid night to close it out.

  • Getting there: a 12–15 min walk north, or Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga → Line 3 to Gyeongbokgung Station (~10 min). Taxi: ~10 min, ≈₩6,000.
  • Morning — Gyeongbokgung. Guard ceremony ≈10:00 and 14:00 (⚠ verify current schedule). Free entry with hanbok rental at gate. Last entry 17:30.
  • Midday — Bukchon Hanok Village. Uphill from the palace — real residents, keep voices low. Spin PokéStops as you wander.
  • Early afternoon — Insadong. Tea houses and craft galleries, ~5 min walk from Bukchon. Iced coffee stop before the next stop.
  • ~15:30 — LoL Park (롤파크). Walk ~10 min south from Insadong to Jonggak (Line 1) Exit 1 — LoL Park is in the Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, right by your base. Open 10:00–19:00. Exhibits trace LCK history from Season 1; Riot store kiosk and LCK shop carry MSI tie-in merch you'll want before Daejeon. Cafe Bilgewater for a drink. Budget 45–60 min. Free entry to exhibits.
  • ~17:00 — head toward Hongdae or any gym-dense area. Tonight is the Road of Legends finale — one of the best raiding evenings of the trip.
  • 18:00–19:00 — Raid Hour: Origin Forme Dialga & Palkia. Best Master League forms (Roar of Time / Spacial Rend), shiny-eligible (1/20), 2–4 trainers. 4–5 passes. See Road of Legends counter charts.
  • 19:00–20:00 — Primal Raid Hour: Primal Kyogre & Groudon. Groudon = quadruple Water weakness, Master League XL Candy, best Xurkitree counter Sunday. 5+ trainers needed — coordinate ahead. 3–4 passes.

Road of Legends ends 23:59 KST. Dinner in Hongdae after 20:00 — dozens of walk-in options right there. Before you subway back to Jongno: T1 Basecamp (마포구 양화로 147, B1) is right by the Hongdae gyms. Half gaming museum, half esports cafe — sit at pro-level setups, order a themed drink, browse the merch shop (open until 22:00). Perfect cap to the night before you head to Daejeon tomorrow.
items.fit: Breathable layers; umbrella doubles for sun and sudden rain.
checklist: Gyeongbokgung open today (closed Tue)
Free entry if you rent a hanbok
Respect Bukchon's quiet hours — it's a residential neighbourhood
18:00–19:00 Raid Hour — Origin Forme Dialga + Palkia (best Master League forms; 4–5 passes)
19:00–20:00 Primal Raid Hour — Primal Kyogre + Primal Groudon (5+ trainers needed; priority Groudon for XL Candy)
Coordinate Primal group beforehand — find raid partners via Pokémon GO chat or Campfire

items.tldr: Morning KTX to Daejeon — GO Fest all day, MSI Lower Bracket Final at 17:00.
items.title: GO Fest Day 1 in Daejeon (all day) → MSI Lower Bracket Final
items.pace: Both of you in Daejeon · morning KTX ≈08:30 → GO Fest 10:00–16:30 EXPO Science Park · LBF 17:00 KST (both inside the arena) · your third is in Tokyo this weekend — see the Tokyo tab

waypoints.name: Jonggak Station — Line 1
waypoints.note: 2 stops to Seoul Station (~5 min)

waypoints.name: Seoul Station — KTX south
waypoints.note: Main Seoul Station only — not Suseo/SRT

waypoints.name: Daejeon Station → Kakao T to I-Hotel
waypoints.note: ~20 min taxi, ≈₩5,500 each split 2

waypoints.name: EXPO Science Park — GO Fest Day 1
waypoints.note: Priority: Mega Mewtwo X (Sat-only debut)

waypoints.name: DCC II — MSI Lower Bracket Final
waypoints.note: NOL World mobile ticket + passport · arena AC layer

items.note: Leaving in the morning instead of afternoon gives you the full 9-hour GO Fest window in Daejeon — all three habitats, no transit cutoff, and you're already 130 m from the arena when the LBF starts.
items.body:
Both of you head south — GO Fest in the morning, then into the arena at 17:00.

  • 08:00 — leave the base. Line 1 from Jonggak, 2 stops to Seoul Station (~5 min, ≈₩1,400) — the central base puts you one short hop from the KTX. Or a quick taxi (~10 min, ≈₩6,000).
  • ~08:30 — KTX to Daejeon. Board the ≈08:30–09:00 KST service south. Main Seoul Station only — not Suseo/SRT. Platform varies; check the Korail app day-of. 50-min ride, arrive Daejeon ≈09:20–09:50.
  • Daejeon Station → I-Hotel. Kakao T taxi (~20 min, ≈₩11,000 — split ≈₩5,500 each). Drop bags at the desk (room available from check-in time; they will hold luggage either way). EXPO Science Park is a 2-min walk east — active gyms and PokéStops across the plaza.

🎮 10:00–16:30 — GO Fest Global Day 1, Daejeon. You have all three Saturday habitats without a transit cutoff:

  • Stormfire Peaks 10:00–13:00 — Ice/Electric/Fire: Lugia, Ho-Oh, Articuno, Zapdos, Moltres, Raikou, Entei, Suicune (5★, all shiny); Mega Blaziken, Mega Ampharos, Mega Abomasnow.
  • Astral Tides 13:00–16:00 — Psychic/Ghost/Water: Dialga, Palkia, Giratina (Altered & Origin), Xerneas, Yveltal, lake trio, Solgaleo, Lunala (5★); Mega Alakazam, Mega Gengar, Mega Swampert.
  • Dragonflight Summit 16:00–19:00 — Flying/Rock/Dragon: Kyogre, Groudon, Rayquaza, Reshiram, Zekrom, Kyurem (5★, all shiny); Mega Salamence, Mega Pidgeot, Mega Aerodactyl. Raid from EXPO Science Park 16:00–16:30, then walk to the arena.

Top priority: Mega Mewtwo X (Super Mega, Saturday-only, global debut) — appears in every habitat. Spend 3–5 of your 9 passes here; fill the rest on Stormfire (Lugia/Ho-Oh XL Candy) and Dragonflight (Rayquaza, Groudon, Kyogre).

  • 16:30 — wrap up, walk to DCC II. 130 m from the hotel. Merch stand before security — Day 1 queue is shorter before 16:45. Bag limit: over 45.7 × 57 × 19 cm = back to hotel first.
  • 17:00 KST — MSI Lower Bracket Final. Passport on you — tickets are name-tied, mobile-only via NOL World app (no screenshots, prints, or transfers). Best-of-5, budget 3–5 hrs. The arena runs aggressive AC — bring a layer.

After the LBF (can run to ~22:00): Cham-i-mat (gamjatang, last order 21:30) or Gobang Sikdang (grill, book ahead) if you wrap by ~21:00; otherwise see Post-match dinner in Where to eat for the Dunsan-dong late-night strip. I-Hotel room is a 2-min walk.

🎮 Your third friend this weekend: he's on a solo Tokyo trip (Jul 11–13), not at GO Fest — his plan is in the Tokyo tab. He flies back Monday and rejoins you in Seoul that night (~20:30).
items.fit: Team colours for the arena; a light layer — DCC II runs fierce AC. Quick-dry shirt for the outdoor EXPO raiding (mid-30s °C).
checklist: Leave the base by 08:00 — Line 1: Jonggak → Seoul Station (2 stops, ~5 min, ≈₩1,400); or a quick taxi (~10 min, ≈₩6,000)
KTX ≈08:30–09:00 Seoul Station → Daejeon — 2 tickets, check platform day-of on Korail app
Kakao T taxi from Daejeon Station to I-Hotel (~20 min, ≈₩5,500 each split 2)
Drop bags at I-Hotel desk; walk to EXPO Science Park by 10:00
9 free Raid Passes — priority: Mega Mewtwo X (debut), then Rayquaza/Groudon/Kyogre in Dragonflight
Wrap raiding by 16:30 — 130 m walk to DCC II for the 17:00 LBF
NOL World app ticket open and tested; passport on you
Battery packs fully charged; arena layer packed
Your third friend is in Tokyo this weekend (Jul 11–13) — his plan is in the Tokyo tab; he rejoins you in Seoul Monday ~20:30

items.tldr: GO Fest Day 2, Grand Final at 15:00 — second Daejeon night, no train to catch.
items.title: GO Fest Day 2 in Daejeon → MSI Grand Final
items.pace: GO Fest 10:00–19:00 Daejeon · Grand Final 15:00 KST · 2nd night in Daejeon (no train to catch)

waypoints.name: EXPO Science Park — GO Fest Day 2
waypoints.note: Mega Mewtwo Y first, then Xurkitree (before 15:00)

waypoints.name: DCC II — MSI Grand Final
waypoints.note: Head over by 14:15 — 2-min walk

waypoints.name: Gobang Sikdang Expo Branch — celebration grill
waypoints.note: Book ahead: 042-863-2104

items.note: Your second Daejeon night removes all the pressure — watch the full Grand Final and still raid Sunday's last habitat.
items.body:
The day the season ends — and with a second Daejeon night, you get to enjoy all of it.

🎮 GO Fest Global Day 2 — Daejeon (10:00–19:00 KST): You're in the Expo/Yuseong district all day. Raid from your hotel area and on the walk to breakfast. EXPO Science Park plazas are the densest PokéStop/gym cluster; Hanbat Arboretum (~1.5 km away) is a backup if you want a change of scenery.

  • Earthforged Domain 10:00–13:00 — Ground/Steel/Normal: Regirock, Regice, Registeel, Regieleki, Regidrago, Regigigas, Heatran, Origin Dialga, Origin Palkia, all Therian/Incarnate formes of the Forces of Nature (5★, all shiny). Mega Metagross, Mega Garchomp, Mega Audino.
  • Verdant Anomaly 13:00–16:00 — Poison/Bug/Grass: Deoxys (all 4 formes), Genesect (all 5 drives), Necrozma, Stakataka, the Tapu quartet, Ultra Beasts. Korea (Asia-Pacific) regional: Xurkitree — only catchable on this continent. Raid it before 15:00. Mega Beedrill, Mega Pinsir, Mega Sceptile.
  • Twilight Battlefield 16:00–19:00 — Dark/Fairy/Fighting: Latias, Latios, Cresselia, Darkrai, Cobalion, Terrakion, Virizion, Zacian, Zamazenta (5★, all shiny). Mega Tyranitar, Mega Gardevoir, Mega Lucario. Overlaps the Grand Final — raid on arena breaks, finish the window after the match (you're staying the night).

Top priority:

  • Mega Mewtwo Y (Super Mega, Sunday-only, global debut, shiny) — every habitat, 3–5 passes
  • Xurkitree (Verdant 13–16, Korea-only UB) — must raid before the 15:00 GF, 2–3 passes
  • Secondary: Therian Landorus/Thundurus + Heatran (Earthforged); Latios + Darkrai (Twilight); Necrozma (Verdant, fusion fodder)

🏟 MSI Grand Final, 15:00 KST. Head over by 14:15 — it's a 2-min walk from the I-Hotel. Best-of-5, likely ends 18:00–20:30. NOL World mobile ticket; passport on you.

Celebrate in Daejeon — both of you, no train to catch. Gobang Sikdang Expo Branch (고반식당, 엑스포로151번길 19, 2F — ~5–10 min walk from I-Hotel; book ahead on 042-863-2104, open Sat–Sun from 12:00) is the premium grill pick. Or head to Dunsan-dong (~15–20 min taxi) for Daejeon's full nightlife strip.
items.fit: Team colours; a layer for arena AC.
checklist: Stay relaxed — second night in Daejeon, no Sunday-night train
GO Fest priority: Mega Mewtwo Y first (Sunday Super Mega only), then Xurkitree
Raid Verdant Anomaly until ~14:15, then arena by 14:15
Twilight Battlefield (Darkrai/Zacian) 16:00–19:00 — raid around the match
Phones and battery packs charged the night before

items.tldr: Slow Daejeon morning, Busan afternoon — markets, beach, milmyeon — home ~22:00.
items.title: Post-MSI: Daejeon → Busan day trip → back to Seoul
items.pace: Relaxed Daejeon morning · KTX to Busan ≈1 hr 25 min · Jagalchi → Gwangalli → KTX back · home by ~22:00

waypoints.name: Sungsimdang DCC (107 Expo-ro)
waypoints.note: Fried soboro + chive bread

waypoints.name: Ossi Kalguksu Doryong
waypoints.note: ⚠ Arrive before 11:00 — fills at lunch

waypoints.name: Daejeon Station — KTX to Busan
waypoints.note: Daejeon Station only — not Daejeon West

waypoints.name: Jagalchi Market
waypoints.note: Cash — small bills for the stalls

waypoints.name: Gukje Market
waypoints.note: 5-min walk from Jagalchi

waypoints.name: Gwangalli Beach
waypoints.note: Gwangan Bridge horizon · beach vendor drinks

waypoints.name: Gaon Milmyeon (109 Minrak Subeon-ro, 2F)
waypoints.note: Last order 20:30 · ⚠ verify open on Naver

waypoints.name: Busan Station — KTX back to Seoul
waypoints.note: Busan Station only — not Gupo/Singyeongju

items.note: The hard-charging weekend is done. Take the Daejeon morning slow — Sungsimdang first, a short walk, lunch, then head to Busan for the afternoon reward. If anyone is running on empty after two GO Fest days and MSI, see the contingency at the bottom and ride straight to Seoul instead.
items.body:
Daejeon delivered. Now spend an afternoon in a city that smells like the sea.

08:00–09:00 — Sungsimdang DCC. Walk from I-Hotel (~2 min) to Sungsimdang DCC Branch (107 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu). Two things: fried soboro (튀김소보로 — sweet crumble bun, deep-fried) and chive bread (부추빵). Queue moves fast. Eat standing outside or on an EXPO bench. Open 08:00.

09:00–09:30 — EXPO / Gapcheon walk. Short decompression loop through the Expo Science Park grounds (15–20 min), then back to check out of I-Hotel. Wide outdoor plazas and waterfront benches — a slow exhale after two hard days. No checklist, just walk.

~09:30 — Ossi Kalguksu Doryong (오씨칼국수 도룡점). 도룡동 4-13, Yuseong-gu, ~5 min walk from I-Hotel. ⚠ Arrive before 11:00 — fills fast once lunch starts. The Doryong branch is the genuine article. Surf-clam broth (동죽조개탕): anchovies + surf clams, mildly spiced, clean. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp. Walk-in only. Naver Map.

~10:30 — Kakao T to Daejeon Station. ≈20 min, ≈₩11,000 split. Daejeon Station only — not Daejeon West. Board the KTX south toward Busan (check Korail app for the 10:30–11:15 departure window). ≈1 hr 25 min to Busan Station. Book the return leg in advance (≈18:30 departure from Busan).

🐟 Busan: Jagalchi + Gukje Market — ≈12:00–13:30.

Kakao T from Busan Station to Jagalchi Market (~15 min, ≈₩9,000–11,000). No itinerary — the market tells you what to do.

  • Each person's mission: one fish cake (어묵), one street snack (hotteok, tteokbokki, or a shellfish cup from an auntie stall). Show her what you want; she handles it. Cash preferred (₩50,000 accepted; small bills easier).
  • Jagalchi Market (남구 자갈치해안로 52, Nam-gu) — indoor building + surrounding outdoor stalls. Open 05:00–22:00 (⚠ closed first and third Tuesday — you're Monday, no issue). Naver Map.
  • Gukje Market (중구 신창동 4가) — 5-min walk from Jagalchi; clothes, household goods, and covered food alleys. No must-buy, but worth a wander. Naver Map.

🌊 Gwangalli Beach — ≈14:00–17:00.

Taxi from Jagalchi to Gwangalli (~25 min, ≈₩12,000–15,000). Drop at Gwanganhaebyeon-ro (광안해변로) — you're at the beach.

  • Walk the shoreline. The Gwangan Bridge (광안대교) fills the horizon. Best for photos at dusk but the wide arc works in any flat July light.
  • Buy iced coffee or watermelon juice from a beach vendor. Sit on the low wall or sand. This is the reward for two days of organized intensity.
  • Optional: Millak Waterside Park (밀락더마켓) is a short walk west — modern food court + sea-facing terrace. Good for a drink break.

🍜 Dinner — Gaon Milmyeon (가온밀면) — ≈17:00.

109 Minrak Subeon-ro (2F), Suyeong-gu, Busan — a 5-min walk or short taxi from Gwangalli Beach. Open 10:00–21:00, last order 20:30. Gwangan Bridge visible from the 2F window. Busan milmyeon (밀면): wheat noodles, lighter than buckwheat naengmyeon, served cold with beef broth or spicy sauce. Locals' favourite; no tourist markup. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp ⚠ — verify price on-site. Naver Map.

~17:30 — taxi to Busan Station for the ≈18:30 KTX back to Seoul.

Busan Suyeong-gu → Busan Station: ≈30–35 min by taxi, ≈₩18,000–22,000. Busan Station only — not Gupo or Singyeongju. KTX north: ≈2 hr 30 min → Seoul Station ≈21:00–21:30. From Seoul Station: Line 1 → Jonggak (2 stops, ~5 min, ≈₩1,400). Or taxi ~10 min, ≈₩6,000. Home by ~22:00. Your third friend flies back from Tokyo the same evening (see the Tokyo tab) — he lands Incheon by ~18:00–18:30 and meets you in Seoul around 20:30, right as you're back from Busan.

⚠ Contingencies

  • If you two are wiped after MSI: skip Busan entirely. Morning KTX direct Daejeon → Seoul (~50 min). Afternoon at Gwangjang Market or DDP, or just rest at the base. The city is still there tomorrow.
  • If running behind schedule: skip the Gapcheon walk + Ossi Kalguksu; taxi straight to Daejeon Station after Sungsimdang. Keep Jagalchi + Gwangalli; drop Gukje Market.
  • If it rains in Busan: swap Gwangalli for Shinsegae Spa Land — Shinsegae Centum City, Centum-daero, Haeundae-gu. Korea's largest department spa; ≈₩22,000 entry; 22 themed sauna rooms, rooftop pool. Busan Metro Line 2 from Busan Station to Centum City Station. Open 06:00–24:00. Naver Map.
items.fit: Walking shoes and cash for Jagalchi stalls. Layer for KTX AC. Keep it light — bags back to the Jongno base tonight.
checklist: Sungsimdang DCC by 08:00 — fried soboro (튀김소보로) + chive bread (부추빵)
Ossi Kalguksu Doryong — arrive before 11:00 (fills early at lunch); ⚠ confirm hours on Naver day-of
Kakao T to Daejeon Station: ≈20 min, ≈₩11,000 total split 2 (≈₩5,500 each); Daejeon Station not Daejeon West
KTX Daejeon → Busan booked (≈10:30 departure); ≈1 hr 25 min
Return KTX Busan → Seoul booked (≈18:30 departure); ≈2 hr 30 min
Cash for Jagalchi stalls — small bills preferred
Gaon Milmyeon dinner ≈17:00 — last order 20:30; 2F beach view (⚠ verify open on Naver)
Taxi from Gwangalli → Busan Station by 17:30 (30–35 min); ≈₩18,000–22,000
Seoul Station → Jonggak via Line 1 (2 stops, ~5 min, ≈₩1,400)

items.tldr: Secret Garden 10:30, noon Apgujeong dash for Stüssy, central K-beauty/K-pop gifts, then the confirmed Flower Blossom send-off.
items.title: The Secret Garden, the big shopping day & a send-off dinner

items.note: Book the 10:30 English Huwon tour ahead (50 online slots per session — it sells out). Dinner is already booked at A Flower Blossom on the Rice — this is your last proper night, and the flight home isn't until 21:30 tomorrow, so linger over it.
items.body:
The palace garden you saved for last, a streetwear run to Apgujeong, K-beauty and K-pop gifts, and the send-off dinner you've already booked.

  • Morning — Changdeokgung & Secret Garden. From the base: a ~12-min walk to Anguk, or Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga → Line 3 one stop to Anguk Station. Arrive 30 min early for the pre-booked 10:30 English Huwon tour (70–90 min guided walk, gentle terrain). Note: the palace itself requires a separate ₩3,000 admission ticket at the gate; the Huwon tour (₩5,000) is on top of that.
  • ~12:00 — noon Apgujeong dash (Stüssy + streetwear). Straight from the Secret Garden: Line 3 from Anguk to Apgujeong (~15 min). Stüssy Seoul Chapter (42 Apgujeong-ro 46-gil, Gangnam; 11:00–20:00) for the merch run, Gentle Monster nearby (art-installation eyewear, free to walk through), and lunch at Kasina Cafe upstairs. This is the one river crossing of the trip — buy here, then drop the bag at the base or a station locker so you're hands-free for the evening. Back central by ~15:00 (Line 3 Apgujeong → Anguk/Jongno).
  • ~15:30 — Myeongdong: K-beauty + K-pop gifts (the for-others run). The central gift cluster, all walkable: Olive Young Myeongdong Town — 2 floors, and crucially it does the tax refund at the register + stores your bags, so the K-beauty gifts ride out with the VAT already knocked off and never weigh you down; then Line Friends Square and the Myeongdong K-pop street for albums, photocards and BT21/NewJeans merch. Keep every receipt. Save the bulk K-beauty for the departure-morning run near the base — today stays light. (Optional if time: Kyobo Book Centre Gwanghwamun for stationery, ~10 min from Insadong.)
  • ~18:30 — A Flower Blossom on the Rice (꽃,밥에피다) — booked ✓, the trip's send-off dinner.
    Where: 3-6 Insadong 16-gil, Jongno-gu — a quiet alley off the main Insadong strip, ~10 min from the base. Michelin Green Star organic Korean. Signature dish: Bojagi Bibimbap — five-coloured vegetables in a thin egg omelet, tied with seaweed, presented with a flower. Warm hospitality; unhurried; not stiff (no dress code to worry about).
    How to get there from Myeongdong: Line 4 to Chungmuro → Line 3 to Anguk Station Exit 6 → ~8-min walk (~20 min total), or a ~10-min taxi (≈₩6,000).
    Reservation: confirmed. Budget ~2 hours — this is calm, unhurried dining, not a meal to rush. If your plans shift, manage the booking via Catch Table Global or +82-2-732-0276.

If plans change — Tuesday alternatives by cuisine (all reservable; CatchTable flagged ✓):

  • Korean BBQ / galbi — Samwon Garden (삼원가든). 835 Eonju-ro, Gangnam, ~7 min from Apgujeong Station, right by Stüssy. Iconic since 1976: Hanwoo galbi grilled over charcoal in a Korean garden. Michelin-listed, relaxed. ≈₩40,000–50,000+ pp. Open Tue, dinner from 17:00. CatchTable ✓ or +82-2-548-3030 — the pick if you'd rather end the night in Apgujeong instead of looping back central.
  • Hanjeongsik — Hanilkwan Apgujeong (한일관). 14 Apgujeong-ro 38-gil, Gangnam — 5 min from Samwon. Since 1939; a brassware course of galbi, bulgogi and banchan. Open daily incl. Tue (11:30–21:30). Book online / +82-1577-9963; ⚠ check CatchTable.
  • Modern Korean — Mishmash (미쉬매쉬). 47 Changdeokgung-gil, Jongno — right by the Secret Garden. Closed Mon, open Tue. ≈₩130,000 pp. CatchTable ✓.
  • Royal court cuisine — Bloom in the Palace Seolleung. Gangnam; 14F city-view court tasting (Mokseokchohwa). ≈₩100,000+ pp; often bookable on shorter notice. CatchTable ✓; ⚠ confirm Tue hours.

(Soseoul Hannam, a common Hannam pick, is closed Mon–Tue — skip it for a Tuesday send-off.)

After dinner — your last night, no early alarm. Insadong and Ikseon-dong's hanok lanes are right at your door for a low-key close. Want a livelier last night instead? Hongdae's still an option: Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga → Line 3 → Euljiro 3-ga → Line 2 → Hongik Univ. (~25 min); the T1 Basecamp merch shop (B1, 147 Yanghwa-ro) closes 22:00. The flight home isn't until 21:30 tomorrow — stay out as late as you like.
items.fit: Walking shoes for the garden paths; comfortable for a long last night out.
checklist: Secret Garden 10:30 English tour booked (50 online slots — sells out)
Today is the main shopping day — Insadong souvenirs, Kyobo stationery, Myeongdong Olive Young (all central & walkable)
A Flower Blossom on the Rice (꽃,밥에피다) dinner pre-booked via Catch Table Global or +82-2-732-0276 — book before the trip
Keep all VAT-refund receipts; passport on you for in-store tax refund
Start packing tonight — tomorrow's departure is deliberately light

items.tldr: Slow Jongno morning, bus 6002 ≈14:30, YP131 home at 21:30.
items.title: Easy departure — slow Jongno morning → limousine bus home
items.pace: Deliberately light final morning · bags sorted early · Airport Limousine 6002 from Gwanghwamun/Jongno 3-ga ≈14:30 · 3+ hr airport buffer · flight YP131 21:30

waypoints.name: Check out the Jongno base (19 Jong-ro)
waypoints.note: Bags: courier to ICN or under the 6002

waypoints.name: Slow Jongno loop — Olive Young Jonggak, Insadong, snacks
waypoints.note: All walkable, no transit

waypoints.name: Bus 6002 stop — Gwanghwamun / Jongno 3-ga
waypoints.note: ₩17,000 pp at the kiosk · ~75 min

waypoints.name: ICN T1 — check-in, VAT refund, YP131 21:30
waypoints.note: 4+ hr cushion · tax-refund kiosk in departures

items.note: Yesterday was the big shopping day, so today stays easy — a slow Jongno morning, luggage handled early, and an unhurried airport run that lands you at ICN with a 3+ hour cushion before the 21:30 flight. No cross-town dashes, no hard stops.
items.body:
Coffee, one last wander, then a comfortable ride to the airport.

  • ~09:30 — check out the Jongno base. Bags packed last night. Two ways to handle luggage: (a) hand them to a same-day airport luggage courier in the morning (Airbnb → ICN departures) so you wander hands-free — book a day ahead, ⚠ confirm operator + cutoff + price; or (b) keep them — the 6002 stows luggage under the bus, so it's easy either way.
  • Late morning — a slow Jongno loop (all walkable from the base, no transit):
    • Olive Young Jonggak — right across the street: the big K-beauty gift haul, saved for last so it goes straight into your bags. Passport out for the in-store tax refund; then hand the load to the luggage courier or check it at ICN — no skincare bag hauled through the heat.
    • Insadong — final souvenir sweep (hanji, tea, ceramics) and Ssamziegil, 5–10 min on foot.
    • Ikseon-dong hanok lanes — one last café or brunch a few minutes east.
    • Snack-souvenir haul at a mart or GS25/CU (honey-butter chips, teas, ramyeon), or save it for ICN duty-free.
  • ~14:30 — Airport Limousine Bus 6002 from Gwanghwamun / Jongno 3-ga → ICN Terminal 1. ₩17,000 pp, buy at the stop kiosk (card). ~75 min; leaving now beats the worst of the evening rush and lands you at ICN ~16:00–17:00 — 4+ hours before the 21:30 flight, exactly the cushion you want. Full stop location + timing in Key transit routes.
  • At ICN T1: Air Premia check-in and bag drop, then the tax-refund kiosk in departures (all stamped receipts), and a last Olive Young / skincare duty-free pass — often cheaper than street price. Plenty of time to eat airside and decompress. YP131 departs T1 at 21:30.

Optional — gaming detour (only if you skipped it Tuesday): if Gunpla, figures, and trading cards at Yongsan i'Park Mall (Animate i'Park inside) are a must, ride Line 1 south to Yongsan in the morning, then back to the base for the bus. It reintroduces cross-town movement — better folded into Tuesday if you can, but the option is here.
items.fit: Travel-comfortable; a layer for the flight. Hands free if you use the luggage courier.
checklist: Check out the Jongno base — bags either sent via luggage courier to ICN or carried on the 6002
Slow Jongno morning: Olive Young Jonggak, Insadong souvenirs, Ikseon-dong café
Airport Limousine 6002 from Gwanghwamun/Jongno 3-ga ≈14:30 → ICN T1 (₩17,000, ~75 min)
Be at ICN by ~17:00 — a 3+ hour buffer before the 21:30 flight
VAT refund kiosk at ICN T1 departures; passport + boarding pass out
Optional Yongsan i'Park (Gunpla/figures) only if you skipped it Tuesday

title: Solo in Seoul — a menu for whenever someone splits off
body:
Two of you are in Daejeon for GO Fest weekend and the third is in Tokyo (see the Tokyo tab), but if anyone peels off from the group on the Seoul days — a rest day, a different pace, someone skipping an activity — this is a menu, not a schedule. Wake up, see how you feel, pick one or two things. Seoul rewards aimless wandering more than almost any city, and it's one of the safest places in the world to explore alone. Everything starts from the Jongno base (Jonggak, Line 1); check exact hours on Naver Map that morning, and keep an indoor option in your pocket (July is monsoon).

To recharge quietly

  • Ikseon-dong & Cheonggyecheon — hanok café lanes and a landscaped stream, both minutes from the base. Café-hop, read by the water, people-watch. The lowest-effort good day in Seoul.
  • Starfield Library, COEX (Samseong, Line 2, ~40 min) — a giant free indoor library atrium; cool, calm, quietly impressive. Pair with the mall + aquarium if it rains. Naver Map.
  • Kyobo Book Centre (Gwanghwamun) or any Hongdae book café — big English sections; hours vanish happily.
  • A second jjimjilbang if Day 1's SPAREX landed well — pure decompression, no conversation required.

For nature and a view (great alone)

  • Inwangsan — a beginner-friendly granite peak (338 m, ~1.5–2 hr round trip) lined with the old fortress wall and skyline views. Start at Gyeongbokgung Station (Line 3) Exit 1, ~10-min walk to the trailhead (~15–20 min from the base). Locals hike it and the wall is lit at night, so it feels safe solo — the clean antidote to GO Fest crowds. Naver Map.
  • Naksan Park + Ihwa Mural Village (Hyehwa, Line 4) — an easy hilltop walk along the city wall; low effort, big payoff near dusk.
  • A Han River park (Ttukseom or Yeouido) — sit by the water with convenience-store ramen, rent a mat for a few thousand won. The most quietly Seoul thing you can do alone.

For art & culture (indoors — heat/rain-proof)

  • National Museum of Korea (Ichon — ~25 min via Seoul Station) — vast and world-class, free permanent collection. Open Sat to 21:00, Sun to 18:00; half a day easy. Naver Map.
  • MMCA Seoul (Samcheong) — contemporary art, central, ≈₩2,000–4,000, open weekends. Leeum (Hannam) — striking modern + traditional collections, ≈₩5,000–10,000, closed Mon and usually needs an online reservation a few days ahead.
  • Deoksugung (City Hall) — a smaller, central palace to take at your own pace; the group's two palace days are different ones, so this stays fresh.

If you're curious about Korea's game culture (no need to be a gamer)

  • LoL Park (Jonggak, Line 1) — the Riot store and exhibits are walk-in; even as a non-player it's a striking slice of Korea's esports world (see Shopping).
  • Hongdae arcades and claw machines, a PC bang for an hour, or Yongsan i'Park Mall (electronics, figures, cards) and Animate (Hongik Exit 4) — all casual and easy solo.

To eat well alone — Korea is mostly solo-friendly, with a couple of exceptions:

  • Easy alone: noodle shops (kalguksu, naengmyeon), gimbap, a counter seat at Gwangjang Market, mall food courts, convenience-store feasts by the river.
  • Harder alone: Korean BBQ and most grills expect two or more — save those for when the group's back.
  • A dessert / café crawl in Yeonnam-dong or the hanok alleys of Ikseon-dong (Jongno-3-ga) is a great low-key afternoon.

Evening, your pace — Hongdae buskers and street food are right outside the door; a film at CGV or Megabox Hapjeong (look for English-subtitled showings); or the Han River at night. If you want company with zero planning, Mike's Cabin and Dokkaebi are walk-in and foreigner-friendly (see Hongdae after dark).

Text the group during the match, and reunite in Seoul when they're back Monday. And if some morning the honest answer is 'sleep in, ramen, then wander Hongdae' — that's a perfect Seoul day too. No pressure to fill it.
